prompt: quick question — in my angi component, can I make the execute function async? like I want to submit to my API when Claude calls the action, not just update local state

assertions.text: Clearly confirms that execute can be async (yes answer given)
Provides a code example with the 'async' keyword on the execute function
Code example includes an actual API call (fetch or similar)
Explains that execute is plain JavaScript — anything valid in a React component works
